I think the "role of the intellectual" should not be to tell people how to build their cities, but to hold up a mirror to the consequences of their actions.
label: LLM

sentence: My job is not to say, "You must be dense to save the planet," but rather, "If you choose to be sprawling, here is the cost you are likely to bear."
label: LLM

sentence: If people decide that the cost of sprawl is worth the autonomy of the garden-city, then that is their democratic and individual prerogative.
label: LLM

sentence: If I call that a "failure," I am no longer a scholar; I am a politician in a lab coat.
